titleMarginInteractive.evidence: The compiled visual state contains Metrics_GroupHeader_TextBlockMargin, but native-group-header-654x242-150pct.png aligns the interactive title with the tile left edge. Applying 8 DIP directly in TileStart's flatter WPF grid produced a measured 12 px offset at 150% DPI.

titleFontSize.evidence: The compiled TextBlock inherits the platform text style; no explicit FontSize is present in GroupHeaderControl.xaml.

titleVerticalAlignment.evidence: At 150% DPI the native title and gripper both center at y=28 in native-group-header-654x242-150pct.png. The previous TileStart bottom alignment centered the title at y=61 while the gripper centered at y=50.

tileStartHeaderToTilesSpacing.evidence: At 150% DPI, native-group-header-654x242-150pct.png places the first tile row about 5 physical pixels below the previous TileStart rendering while preserving the verified 32-DIP header height and centered title baseline. This is a TileStart compensation, not a compiled XBF margin.

verifiedBehavior.fact: The resting header uses a TextBlock. A separate NameTextBoxHost is populated only while editing; the stock template does not use a permanently visible transparent TextBox.
Tapping the group header enters edit mode. Enter commits and exits; Escape exits without committing; lost focus commits and exits.
An unnamed customizable group is blank at rest. Pointer interaction selects FocusState_HoverPlaceholder and displays TileGrid_UnnamedGroupHint with the placeholder hover brush.
Pointer handlers and capture are registered on GroupHeaderControl itself. The 48-DIP gripper is a hover/pressed affordance, not the only drag hit target.
Group rearrangement is transactional: begin creates TileGroupLayoutState, preview updates the group location, drop commits, and cancel reapplies the saved state.

The native group resolver returns a two-dimensional target cell. It resolves a group column from X, uses the target group's vertical midpoint to choose its top or the cell after its bottom, and uses GetLastOccupiedCellInColumn to return lastRow + 1 when the pointer is in empty space at the end of a column.

unresolved: The exact desktop mouse drag threshold is encapsulated by the native MouseGestureProvider. TileStart uses the Windows system drag distance until runtime tracing provides the internal value.

The exact native brush colors for placeholder hover, pressed, and drag depend on theme resources. The same-scale pointer-over reference shows no visible full-width hover fill, so TileStart keeps the ordinary pointer-over surface transparent while retaining accent feedback for pressed and drag states.
The exact group displacement easing is not yet isolated from the native theme transition. TileStart initially reuses the already calibrated Win10 reorder spline and requires Win10 runtime comparison.

The native vertical group CellLocation quantum and empty-cell persistence rules are not yet isolated. TileStart persists column/row order and stacks each column by the measured group height, so inserting a group shifts only later groups in that column without claiming the native layout uses the same height accumulator.
